metadata.purpose: Group candidates by adoption shape so rankings are interpreted within comparable tool classes.

groups.name: Programmable SDK or framework
groups.use_when: The goal is to build a product-specific orchestrator or agent workflow.
groups.watch_for: The team must still design policy, tools, telemetry, deployment, and failure handling.

groups.name: Local developer CLI or assistant
groups.use_when: The goal is quick developer productivity, local workflow, or lightweight automation.
groups.watch_for: Local convenience does not automatically provide autonomous-execution safety.

groups.name: Secure PR automation
groups.use_when: The goal is controlled autonomous or semi-autonomous issue-to-PR work.
groups.watch_for: Sandboxing, network policy, credentials, branch protection, and review artifacts are mandatory.

groups.name: Research harness
groups.use_when: The goal is reproducible experiments, ablations, or SWE-bench-style evaluations.
groups.watch_for: Benchmark fit is not the same as enterprise workflow fit.

groups.name: Control plane or multi-backend layer
groups.use_when: The goal is to coordinate multiple backends, users, queues, policies, and artifacts.
groups.watch_for: Operational ownership and integration scope can dominate framework selection.

groups.name: Experimental reference
groups.use_when: The goal is to track design ideas or run exploratory spikes.
groups.watch_for: High evidence risk or alpha maturity prevents first-phase production adoption.
